title_en: Hadiths on Purification, Prayer, and Fasting
content_en:
The Sunan of Abu Dawud opens with the Book of Purification (Kitab at-Taharah), and al-Azimabadi's commentary on this section is among the most detailed portions of the Awn al-Ma'bud. The legal importance of purification as the prerequisite for prayer means that the hadiths in this section carry enormous practical weight, and al-Azimabadi treats them with corresponding thoroughness.

The famous hadith about the water that remains pure unless its color, taste, or smell changes received extended treatment in the commentary. Al-Azimabadi traces the debate among hadith scholars about the chains of this narration and its precise wording, then moves to the jurisprudential implications: the Shafi'i and Hanbali schools accepted this criterion for judging when water becomes ritually impure (najis), while the Hanafi school relied on a different set of principles involving quantity thresholds. Al-Azimabadi presents both positions with their textual foundations.

The hadiths on wudu (ablution) and ghusl (full ritual bath) are treated with similar thoroughness. Al-Azimabadi explains the disagreements over whether wiping the head (mash al-ra's) must cover the entire head or may be limited to a portion, citing the hadiths supporting each position and the reasoning of the scholars who adopted them. His commentary on the hadiths dealing with the prayer of the traveler (qasr as-salah) — shortening four-unit prayers to two — is notable for its detailed survey of the minimum travel distance that triggers this permission.

On fasting, Sunan Abi Dawud contains important narrations about the beginning and ending of Ramadan, the conditions for the validity of the fast, and the acts that break or merely diminish it. Al-Azimabadi's commentary addresses the famous hadith permitting the traveler to break the fast in Ramadan, examining the conditions under which this permission applies and whether fasting while traveling is actually dispreferred or merely sub-optimal.

The sections on supererogatory worship — voluntary fasts, night prayer, and dhikr — also receive careful attention. Al-Azimabadi draws out the prophetic hadiths emphasizing the spiritual benefits of these practices while being careful to maintain the distinction between obligatory and recommended worship.
title_id: Hadis-Hadis tentang Bersuci, Salat, dan Puasa
content_id:
Sunan Abi Dawud dibuka dengan Kitab al-Thaharah, dan syarah al-Azimabadi atas bagian ini termasuk bagian yang paling rinci dari Awn al-Ma'bud. Pentingnya bersuci secara hukum sebagai prasyarat salat berarti hadis-hadis dalam bagian ini memiliki bobot praktis yang sangat besar, dan al-Azimabadi memperlakukannya dengan ketelitian yang setara.

Hadis terkenal tentang air yang tetap suci kecuali warna, rasa, atau baunya berubah mendapat perlakuan yang panjang dalam syarah. Al-Azimabadi menelusuri perdebatan di kalangan ulama hadis tentang sanad-sanad riwayat ini dan redaksi tepatnya, kemudian beralih ke implikasi-implikasi fikih: mazhab Syafi'i dan Hanbali menerima kriteria ini untuk menilai kapan air menjadi najis secara ritual, sementara mazhab Hanafi mengandalkan seperangkat prinsip berbeda yang melibatkan ambang kuantitas. Al-Azimabadi menyajikan kedua posisi beserta landasan-landasan tekstualnya.

Hadis-hadis tentang wudu dan ghusl diperlakukan dengan ketelitian yang serupa. Al-Azimabadi menjelaskan ketidaksepakatan tentang apakah mengusap kepala (mash al-ra's) harus mencakup seluruh kepala atau dapat dibatasi pada sebagian, mengutip hadis-hadis yang mendukung setiap posisi dan penalaran para ulama yang mengadopsinya. Syarahnya atas hadis-hadis yang berkaitan dengan salat musafir (qasr al-shalah) — mempersingkat salat empat rakaat menjadi dua — terkenal karena survei terperincinya tentang jarak perjalanan minimum yang memicu izin ini.

Tentang puasa, Sunan Abi Dawud mengandung riwayat-riwayat penting tentang awal dan akhir Ramadan, syarat-syarat keabsahan puasa, dan perbuatan-perbuatan yang membatalkan atau hanya menguranginya. Syarah al-Azimabadi membahas hadis terkenal yang mengizinkan musafir untuk berbuka puasa di Ramadan, memeriksa kondisi-kondisi di mana izin ini berlaku dan apakah berpuasa dalam perjalanan sebenarnya dimakruhkan atau sekadar kurang optimal.

Bagian-bagian tentang ibadah sunnah — puasa sukarela, salat malam, dan dzikir — juga mendapat perhatian yang cermat. Al-Azimabadi mengungkap hadis-hadis kenabian yang menekankan manfaat-manfaat spiritual dari amalan-amalan ini sambil berhati-hati mempertahankan perbedaan antara ibadah wajib dan yang dianjurkan.
content_ar: The Sunan of أبو داود opens with the الكتاب of Purification (Kitab at-الطهارة), and al-Azimabadi's commentary on this الفصل is among the most detailed portions of the Awn al-Ma'bud. The legal importance of purification as the prerequisite for الصلاة means that the hadiths في هذا الفصل carry enormous practical weight, and al-Azimabadi treats them with corresponding thoroughness. The famous الحديث about the water that remains pure إلا إذا its color, taste, or smell changes received extended treatment in the commentary. Al-Azimabadi traces the debate among الحديث العلماء about the chains of this narration and its precise wording, then moves to the jurisprudential implications: the الشافعي and الحنبلي schools accepted this criterion for judging عندما water becomes ritually impure (نجس), بينما the الحنفي school relied on a different set of principles involving quantity thresholds. Al-Azimabadi presents both positions with their textual foundations. The hadiths on الوضوء (ablution) and الغسل (full ritual bath) are treated with similar thoroughness. Al-Azimabadi explains the disagreements over whether wiping the head (mash al-ra's) must cover the entire head or may be limited to a portion, citing the hadiths supporting each position and the reasoning of the العلماء who adopted them. His commentary on the hadiths dealing with the الصلاة of the traveler (qasr as-الصلاة) — shortening four-unit الصلوات to two — is notable for its detailed survey of the minimum travel distance that triggers this permission. On الصيام, Sunan Abi داود ﷺ contains المهم narrations about the beginning and ending of Ramadan, the conditions for the validity of the fast, and the acts that break or merely diminish it. Al-Azimabadi's commentary addresses the famous الحديث permitting the traveler to break the fast in Ramadan, examining the conditions under which this permission applies and whether الصيام بينما traveling is actually dispreferred or merely sub-optimal. The sections on supererogatory worship — voluntary fasts, night الصلاة, and الذكر — أيضاً receive careful attention. Al-Azimabadi draws out the prophetic hadiths emphasizing the spiritual benefits of these practices بينما being careful to maintain the distinction between obligatory and recommended worship.
